name: postmortem
description: Incident postmortem pipeline: timeline → analysis → action items
headline: Write a postmortem from logs/PRs/chat — 2 agents (no blameless box-ticking)

agents.name: researcher
agents.description: Build the timeline from available evidence
agents.prompt:
INCIDENT: {{task}}

Reconstruct the timeline. Read git log, recent PRs, error logs, any incident chat threads, anything in {{cwd}} that bears on this.

Write to {{outputPath}}:

## TL;DR (2 sentences)
## Impact
- users affected
- duration
- data integrity

## Timeline
(timestamped, factual, no opinions)

## Evidence used
(commits, logs, files)

agents.name: writer
agents.description: Write the postmortem with root causes and action items
agents.prompt:
INCIDENT: {{task}}

RESEARCH:
{{researcher}}

Write the postmortem. Style: blameless but not toothless. Real action items, owners, due dates.

Write to {{outputPath}}:

# Postmortem: <incident>

## TL;DR
## Impact
## Timeline
(reuse from researcher)

## Root causes
(systemic, not 'X made a typo' — what allowed the typo to ship?)

## What went well
## What went poorly
## Action items
| owner | item | due | issue |

## Lessons
(2-4 short, memorable rules)

Do NOT include 'be more careful' or 'pay better attention'. Those aren't action items.
